reason: Both correctly identify the core move: drop "AI-powered PM," lead with the forecasting outcome, and treat PM features as supporting proof. Answer B states this crisply and correctly but stops there — one paragraph, no elaboration on why it works, no discussion of the buyer/competitive shift, and no concrete execution steps. Answer A makes the same core call but goes further in ways that add real decision-relevant value: it explains the categories-vs-capability logic, connects utilization to agency P&L/margin (a sharper buyer insight), correctly reframes the competitive set (Float/Runn instead of Asana/Monday), and gives concrete tactical moves (homepage headline, demo order, benchmark report, coexistence strategy) plus a clear litmus test for success. None of this is padding — each point adds substance a busy exec could act on. A is longer but earns the length with density, not hedging.
